expected_failure_modes: Agent may hover indefinitely if distance penalty is too small to drive descent, but the vertical speed penalty near ground and fuel cost push toward completing the landing.
With too little progress weight, the agent might learn to freeze at a high altitude if it minimizes stability penalties there; but the distance penalty ensures stationary high altitude is costly.
Hard-landing penalty (large negative) might discourage any attempt to land if the success event’s low-speed threshold is too strict; tuning thresholds and reward magnitudes is needed.
risk_notes: Stability quality might subtly compete with progress during fast initial descent; the stability penalty on vx and angle is mild and should not overcome progress pressure.
Action penalty uses absolute values; side_engine is signed, so abs(side_engine) correctly penalizes both directions equally.

Event rules are one‑shot per episode because contacts are only transiently gained; bouncing could trigger multiple event firings if contacts oscillate, potentially yielding repeated rewards/penalties – typical environment resets contacts only once, but if contacts can toggle, the rule may fire multiple times.
The condition `y < 0.5` for vertical speed penalty assumes the ground is at y=0 and the lander’s y coordinate is the height above ground; this must align with the environment’s coordinate system.
